name: 01_happy_path
description: Candidate joins with their real name, one interviewer. Baseline sanity check.

data.text: Hi Rahul, thanks for joining! I'm Priya from the platform team. Could you start by telling me about yourself?

Sure, thanks Priya. I'm Rahul, I've been a backend engineer for about five years. In my current role at Zenpay I led the payments reconciliation service, and before that I worked at a logistics startup where I built their order tracking system from scratch.
Great. Can you walk me through the architecture of that reconciliation service?

Of course. My team owned three services. I designed the ingestion layer around Kafka so we could replay events, and the matching engine ran as a stateless consumer group. We kept settlement state in Postgres with an outbox pattern for downstream notifications.
How would you handle a poison message in that pipeline?
We hit exactly that in production. I implemented a dead letter topic with bounded retries and an alerting hook, so operators could inspect and re-drive messages after a fix.
Let's move on to a short coding exercise. Can you share your screen? Oh great, you already have.
Sure, I'll start with a brute force approach and then optimise. So first I'll parse the input into a map keyed by user id.
